campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and overall spending. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, focusing on different ways people can get around the city.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasurer's monthly financial report
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan - improving transit, biking, and walking options

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 where residents could voice their opinions on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing public comments, the council introduced the ordinance and decided to fast-track it by skipping the usual first reading process, planning to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on proposed ordinance
First reading of ordinance waived to speed up adoption process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular council meeting
Residents had opportunity to comment before council decision

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve official records from several meetings held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting focused on administrative housekeeping to keep the city's records up to date.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six March 2026 meetings
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Routine administrative and record-keeping matters

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize and finalize their upcoming agendas for April 21 and April 28, 2026. They reviewed what items to add, which topics need specific time slots, and any legal notice requirements. The council also reviewed public records from early April and discussed a VEBA Advisory Committee matter.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 21, 2026 meeting agenda
Planned the April 28, 2026 draft agenda with new items
Assigned time slots to specific agenda items
Reviewed public records from April 2-9, 2026
Discussed VEBA Advisory Committee matters

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to review the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including major projects and improvements planned for 2026-27. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program budget review
Teen Month proclamation
National Volunteer Week recognition
City infrastructure and project planning

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review previous meeting notes and discuss changes to tenant protection rules in Sunnyvale. The main focus was introducing a new ordinance that would update Chapter 19.71 of the city's housing code, though specific details of what's changing weren't provided in this agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
Proposed amendments to tenant protections program (Chapter 19.71)
Updates to residential renter safeguards and rights

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026, where residents were invited to speak about any local issues for up to 3 minutes each. The council also held elections for chair and vice chair positions and started discussing the upcoming fiscal year budget.
keyTopics: Public comment period for residents on any city matters
Chair and Vice Chair elections for the council
Fiscal year budget development and planning

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal information about deadlines for residents who want to challenge city decisions in court. If you disagree with a city decision, you have 90 days from when that decision becomes final to file a lawsuit, or you lose your right to challenge it.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over decisions
Legal disclosure requirements for quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 requirements
How to protect your legal rights when disputing city actions

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to discuss updates to the city's long-term housing and community development plans for 2025-2030, review new project requests from council members, and consider changes to how the city fills council vacancies. The council also approved previous meeting notes and previewed upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Updates to 2025-2030 housing and community development funding plans
New project requests proposed by individual council members
Changes to council vacancy filling procedures
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is asking to officially recognize it as a historic building and get permission to add 5,000 square feet of new floor space as part of seismic safety improvements.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade from Category 4 to Category 2
Request for 5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Downtown Commercial zone property with pedestrian shopping district rules
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
